issuesFound.title: Image missing alt attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The `` element with `src="/img/luminary-logo.svg"` lacks an alt attribute, which prevents screen readers from describing the logo to users. This blocks access for visually impaired users relying on screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 105: ``

issuesFound.title: Missing accessible name on link
issuesFound.explanation: The `` element with `href="/products"` lacks a visible text name, aria-label, or aria-labelledby. The link text "Products" is not visible due to the `` element being the sole content and having no alt text. This blocks access for screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 105: `Products`

issuesFound.title: Missing accessible name on button
issuesFound.explanation: The `<button>` with `aria-label="Open navigation menu"` has no visible text content, which means it lacks an accessible name. Screen readers will announce only the aria-label, but the button itself has no text to describe its action.
issuesFound.fix: Line 101: `<button type="button" aria-label="Open navigation menu" class="nav-toggle">Open navigation menu</button>`

issuesFound.title: Non-descriptive link text
issuesFound.explanation: The `<a>` element with `href="/signup"` inside the hero CTA has the text "Start your free 14-day trial" but only the last part ("14-day trial") is meaningful. The phrase "Start your" is redundant and does not convey the action's purpose to screen reader users. This significantly degrades the link's accessibility.
issuesFound.fix: Line 105: `Start free trial`

issuesFound.title: Missing heading level skip
issuesFound.explanation: The heading hierarchy skips from `<h1>` to `<h2>` (hero section) but then jumps to `<h3>` (features section) without an intermediate heading. This creates a skip of two levels, which can confuse screen reader users navigating the page.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 101: Add `

` between hero and features sections, e.g., `

Feature Overview

`

issuesFound.title: Missing accessible label for nav
issuesFound.explanation: The `<nav>` element with `id="main-nav"` lacks an `aria-label` or `aria-labelledby`. Since there is no parent heading that can be referenced, this landmark is not properly labeled, which can confuse screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 101: `<nav id="main-nav" aria-label="Primary navigation menu">`

issuesFound.title: Missing heading scope for table
issuesFound.explanation: The `<th>` elements in the feature comparison table lack `scope` attributes for the rows.
issuesFound.fix: Line 163: `Projects3UnlimitedUnlimited` *(Note: The table structure is already correct for scope, but the `` elements in the header row need scope attributes for the first row of data.)*

issuesFound.title: Missing accessible label for form input
issuesFound.explanation: The `<input>` element with `id="search-input"` lacks a `<label>` or `aria-label`/`aria-labelledby`. This violates WCAG 2.2 by not associating the input with its label.
issuesFound.fix: Line 109: `<input id="search-input" type="search" name="q" placeholder="Search docs, blog, tools…" aria-label="Search Luminary Studio">`

issuesFound.title: Missing autocomplete attribute for personal data input
issuesFound.explanation: The `<input>` element with `name="first_name"` suggests collecting personal data (name) but lacks the `autocomplete` attribute. This violates WCAG 1.3.5 by not specifying how personal data should be handled.
issuesFound.fix: Line 110: `<input id="contact-first-name" type="text" name="first_name" autocomplete="given-name" required aria-required="true" aria-label="First name">`

issuesFound.title: Missing required attribute for form input
issuesFound.explanation: The `<input>` element with `id="newsletter-consent"` lacks the `required` attribute, which is necessary for the checkbox to enforce user input. This violates WCAG 3.3.2 by not ensuring required fields are marked.
issuesFound.fix: Line 117: `<input id="newsletter-consent" type="checkbox" name="consent" required aria-required="true" aria-label="Mark to receive marketing emails">`

issuesFound.title: Missing aria-controls reference
issuesFound.explanation: The `<button>` with `aria-controls="solutions-submenu"` references an element (`id="solutions-submenu"`) that does not exist in the document. This violates WCAG 2.2 by having a broken ARIA reference.
issuesFound.fix: Line 103: `<button type="button" aria-controls="solutions-submenu" aria-label="Solutions menu">Solutions</button>` *(Note: The menu exists, but the issue is the button text is hidden by `aria-hidden="true"` on the parent button. The referenced element is valid.)*

issuesFound.title: Missing aria-expanded attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The `<button>` with `aria-controls="nav-menu"` is a toggle button that controls expandable content but lacks the `aria-expanded` attribute. This violates SC 4.1.2 by not indicating its current state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 101: `<button type="button" aria-expanded="false" aria-controls="nav-menu" aria-label="Open navigation menu" class="nav-toggle">☰</button>`
